description: Working on the customer authentication and account lifecycle for both consumer and partner channels, plus the wider reservation platform around it.
items: Built the customer authentication and account lifecycle on Kotlin/Spring Boot with Keycloak / Red Hat SSO — account creation, email verification, password reset, login, logout, account recovery, profile updates, account deletion, and email-based MFA.
Built the token and API-security layer — JWT issuance and validation, refresh-token rotation, token introspection, silent token renewal, and API gateway integration.

Set up the B2B identity surface — partner and corporate account provisioning, with role and permission management inside partner organisations — running through a single Keycloak instance shared with B2C.
Worked across the wider reservation platform alongside the auth work — Kotlin microservices on Spring Boot, hexagonal/DDD, deployed on AWS through GitLab CI/CD.
Rewrote slow PostgreSQL queries on the auth and account services and added composite indexes where lookups were doing full table scans.
results: Modernised the team's Gradle build setup with multi-level CI caching, cutting build times by ~50% (≈12 min → ≈5 min) — pattern adopted as the internal reference by 3+ adjacent JVM teams.

Authored a C4-model architecture POC using Structurizr DSL and presented it to the team as a proposal for living architecture documentation; adopted by individual developers interested in learning the DSL.

description: Short engagement building a data quality tool that scanned Dassault's global online sales and product-referential systems for missing fields, broken references, and inconsistent values across millions of records.
items: Started as a Python + SQL prototype on the internal stack, then rewrote it as a Java/Spring Boot service so it could plug into the regular release pipeline.
Worked with business analysts to turn vague data-quality rules into concrete checks the release process could run on its own.

company.name: Audi AG, Ingolstadt — via Lunatech SAS

description: Built an in-house labeling platform that produced ground-truth video data for Audi's autonomous-driving model training. Worked remotely from Paris on a fast-growing team.
items: Set the backend architecture early on, applying Play Framework's architectural patterns to the Akka-HTTP codebase to give it the structure and conventions a fast-growing team would need.
Wrote the React + ScalaJS frontends used by the labelers, wired directly into the labeling backend.
Set up role-based access with Forgerock IAM across the labeling tools and admin surfaces.
Packaged services in Docker, ran them on GKE, and integrated with Cloud TPU and Cloud AI Platform so model predictions could be used to pre-label data and speed up annotation.
results: That early architectural call paid off as the team grew — multiple developers came onto the project later and could ship features quickly without spending time fighting the codebase or relitigating structure.

description: Co-led the technical pilot for D4th, the new marketing platform integrating Tridion CMS with the Disneyland Paris public site (~10 million annual visitors).
items: The pilot landed well enough that Disney funded a multi-million-euro programme on the back of it to fully replace the legacy marketing application.
Wrote supporting microservices for browser push notifications and the SOAP integration with the older internal systems.
results: Took on GitLab CI/CD setup and production deployment myself, even though both were outside my original scope — figured out the existing infrastructure, ran the initial deployments, and handed it off cleanly when a more experienced infra engineer joined.

description: Worked remotely on a B2B shipment-lifecycle platform — new features, maintenance, and day-to-day operations.
items: Added a Quartz-based scheduler that pushed automated reports to operations so delivery issues could be caught earlier.
Deployed to AWS EC2 and handled day-to-day production: log analysis and incident triage.

company.name: Nokia Bell Labs (formerly Alcatel-Lucent) — Paris, France

description: Internship in the research division working on video-quality estimation and streaming protocols.
items: Built a video-quality estimation tool in Java implementing the ITU-T P.1201.1 model.
Analysed streaming traffic with Wireshark and tcpdump as part of research into QUIC, HTTP/2, and H.264.
